title: Word Formation
description: Practice forming adjectives from numbers/nouns, verbs, prefixes, suffixes, and -ever words.

title: The Future in the Past
description: Practice using was/were going to, past progressive, and would to describe future events from a past perspective.
grammar: was/were going to, past progressive, would, will/would

title: Spelling and Punctuation
description: Practice capitalization, possessive forms, plurals, doubling consonants, dropping final -e, homonyms, and punctuation.

title: Simple Present vs. Present Progressive
description: Practice distinguishing between simple present and present progressive, including non-action verbs and frequency adverbs.
grammar: Simple Present, Present Progressive, Frequency Adverbs

title: Past and Perfect Tenses
description: Practice simple past, past progressive, present perfect, already/yet, for/since, and used to.
grammar: Simple Past, Past Progressive, Present Perfect, used to

title: Prepositions and Idioms
description: Practice prepositions of time, prepositions after adjectives, verbs followed by prepositions, and idioms with up, down, over, out.

title: Phrasal Verbs
description: Practice separable and inseparable phrasal verbs, three-word phrasal verbs, and nouns derived from phrasal verbs.

title: Modal Auxiliary Verbs
description: Practice modals for ability, permission, advisability, obligation, possibility, and their progressive and past forms.
grammar: Modal Verbs (can, could, may, might, should, must, have to)

title: Making Comparisons
description: Practice comparative and superlative structures with adjectives and adverbs, and modifying comparisons.

title: Connecting Ideas
description: Practice linking phrases for purpose, reason, contrast, and relative/interrogative clauses.
grammar: Linking Phrases, Relative Clauses, Interrogative Clauses

title: Idioms and Idiomatic Expressions
description: Practice pair idioms, similes, idioms with colors/numbers/animals/body parts, and uses of get, take, make, and do.

title: Causatives
description: Practice active and passive forms of causative verbs like have, let, make, and get.

title: Gerunds and Infinitives
description: Practice verbs followed by gerunds or infinitives, gerunds after prepositions, and infinitives with too/enough.

title: Future Forms
description: Practice simple future (will vs. going to), future progressive, future perfect, and future perfect progressive.
grammar: will, going to, Future Progressive, Future Perfect
